response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished Staff/Senior Software Engineer with over 14 years of experience in web, backend, and embedded software development, but the role of Principal Performance Architect requires deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload analysis at the silicon level. Nothing in his resume — including his current Google role focused on TypeScript, AI-agent tooling, and MCP frameworks — demonstrates any exposure to hardware architecture, chip design, SIMD/MIMD paradigms, or low-level DL workload performance profiling. His low-latency software work at Sociomantic is the closest signal, but it operates entirely in the software domain. The skill gap is fundamental, not bridgeable with ramp-up time.
strengths: Over 14 years of progressive seniority, currently at Staff level at Google, satisfying the experience threshold for a BS holder
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering through low-latency, high-throughput microservices work at Sociomantic using async I/O and high-performance databases
Active involvement in AI-adjacent infrastructure at Google (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) showing awareness of the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero background in SoC or IP modeling — the primary technical requirement for this role — with no hardware architecture work anywhere in the resume
No experience with GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD parallelism, or AI accelerator design, which are the core preferred qualifications
AI involvement is entirely at the software/tooling abstraction layer (agent frameworks, workflows), not at the deep-learning workload or hardware optimization level required here

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No GPU/AI accelerator or DL workload optimization background
Seniority level and software engineering depth
